subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Pawns
- **1** scoring board (inside the box)
- **6** wooden rabbits (rabbeeples)

#### Cards
- **84** image cards

#### Voting Tokens
- **36** voting tokens
- **6 colors** of tokens
- **Number range** — Tokens are numbered 1 to 6

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Core Table Setup
- **Score track** — Place the board where all players can see the scoring track.
- **Card deck** — Shuffle all 84 cards into one deck.
- **Starting hands** — Deal 6 cards to each player (unless using 3-player variant).
- **Draw pile** — Put remaining cards face down as a draw pile.
- **Pawn start** — Place each rabbit on space 0.

#### Display Area Each Turn
- **Reveal row** — Storyteller will reveal submitted cards in one row.
- **Numbering rule** — Number cards from left to right: 1, 2, 3, and so on.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### All Players
- **Choose pawn** — Take 1 rabbit and place it on score 0.
- **Keep hand hidden** — Do not show your cards to other players.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Starting hand** — 6 cards per player.
- **Voting tokens** — Each player takes tokens 1-4.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Starting hand** — 6 cards per player.
- **Voting tokens** — Each player takes tokens 1-5.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Starting hand** — 6 cards per player.
- **Voting tokens** — Each player takes tokens 1-6.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Starting hand** — 7 cards per player.
- **Card submission** — Each non-storyteller submits 2 cards instead of 1.
- **Displayed cards** — 5 cards are displayed for voting.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 — Player-Count Reference List (Rulebook Audit)
- **Found variation** — 4-player voting token range differs from 5-player and 6-player.
- **Found variation** — 3-player variant changes starting hand size.
- **Found variation** — 3-player variant changes number of submitted cards per non-storyteller.
- **Found variation** — 3-player variant changes one scoring case (only one player finds storyteller card).
- **Found variation** — Core game uses 4/5/6-player token ranges in setup.

#### First Storyteller
- **Selection rule** — First player to describe one of their cards aloud becomes first storyteller.

#### First Clue
- **Clue formats** — A clue can be one word, multiple words, a sound, or a reference (movie, proverb, lyric, etc.).
- **Hidden card** — Storyteller chooses one card from hand and does not reveal it.

#### First Submissions
- **Non-storytellers** — Each non-storyteller chooses a card matching the clue and submits it face down to storyteller (except 3-player variant, where each submits 2 cards).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Clue step** — Storyteller selects 1 hand card and says a clue aloud.
2. **Submission step** — Each non-storyteller submits matching card(s) face down.
3. **Shuffle step** — Storyteller shuffles all submitted cards with their own card.
4. **Reveal step** — Storyteller reveals cards in a row and numbers them left to right.
5. **Vote step** — Non-storytellers choose 1 numbered token and vote face down.
6. **Reveal votes** — Flip all votes and place each vote on its chosen card.
7. **Score step** — Award points based on storyteller result and decoy votes.
8. **Refill step** — Each player draws back up to full hand size for that mode.
9. **Pass storyteller** — Player to current storyteller’s left becomes next storyteller.

#### Clockwise Flow
- **Turn order** — Storyteller role always moves clockwise after each turn.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Storyteller Actions
- **Choose card** — Select exactly 1 card from your hand as storyteller card.
- **Give clue** — Say clue before any reveal.
- **Clue style** — Use words, sounds, or references allowed by the rules.
- **No vote** — Storyteller does not vote in that turn.

#### Non-Storyteller Actions
- **Choose decoy** — Pick the card that best matches storyteller clue.
- **Submit hidden** — Give selected card(s) face down to storyteller.
- **Secret vote** — Vote with numbered token corresponding to one displayed card.
- **Vote restriction** — You may not vote for your own submitted card.

#### Table Actions
- **Card numbering** — Leftmost revealed card is 1, next is 2, etc.
- **Point movement** — Move rabbits forward by points scored each turn.
- **Hand refill** — Draw to hand limit after scoring (6 in core game, 7 in 3-player variant).

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Core Scoring Tension
- **Sweet spot clue** — Best clues are neither too obvious nor too obscure.
- **Failure case A** — If everyone finds storyteller card, storyteller scores 0.
- **Failure case B** — If nobody finds storyteller card, storyteller scores 0.
- **Decoy reward** — Non-storytellers score +1 for each vote on their submitted card.

#### Voting Scale by Player Count
--- 4 Players ---
- **Token range** — Use tokens 1-4.
- **Display size** — 4 cards displayed (storyteller + 3 submissions).

--- 5 Players ---
- **Token range** — Use tokens 1-5.
- **Display size** — 5 cards displayed (storyteller + 4 submissions).

--- 6 Players ---
- **Token range** — Use tokens 1-6.
- **Display size** — 6 cards displayed (storyteller + 5 submissions).

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Hand size** — 7 cards per player.
- **Submission count** — Each non-storyteller submits 2 cards.
- **Display size** — 5 cards displayed.
- **Scoring tweak** — If only one player finds storyteller card, storyteller and that player score 4 (instead of 3).

#### Optional Presentation Variant
- **Mimes/songs mode** — Storyteller may mime or sing clue relation.
- **Rule continuity** — All other rules remain unchanged.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Scoring (Core Game)
- **All or none found** — Storyteller scores 0; every non-storyteller scores 2.
- **Some found** — Storyteller scores 3; each correct voter scores 3.
- **Decoy bonus** — Each non-storyteller scores +1 per vote on their card.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Only one finder case** — Storyteller and the sole correct finder score 4 instead of 3.

#### End Trigger
- **Deck depletion** — Game ends when the last card from the draw pile has been drawn.

#### Winner
- **Highest position** — Player farthest along scoring track wins.
- **Tie note** — No official tie-breaker is stated in the provided excerpt.
- **Table fallback** — Use shared victory or agreed house tie-break if tied.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Calibrate clues early** — In your first storyteller turn, aim for 1-2 correct guesses, not all and not zero.
- **Track player taste** — Note who interprets clues literally vs metaphorically by turn 2.
- **Submit believable decoys** — Pick cards likely to steal votes from at least one opponent.
- **Preserve flexibility** — Keep at least one broad-interpretation card in hand for future storyteller turns.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Pair your submissions** — Submit two cards that pull guesses in different directions, not two similar decoys.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-literal clue** — Everyone identifies storyteller card and storyteller scores 0.
- **Over-abstract clue** — Nobody identifies storyteller card and storyteller scores 0.
- **Aesthetic voting** — Picking favorite art instead of clue fit loses repeat points.
- **Weak decoy choice** — Playing a decoy that can’t attract votes wastes +1 opportunities.
- **Own-card tunnel vision** — Forgetting self-vote is illegal leads to rushed bad votes.

--- 3 Players (Variant) ---
- **Duplicate decoy role** — Submitting two near-identical cards makes both easy to eliminate.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Clarity vs ambiguity** — Decide how close your clue should be to target card to avoid both zero-score failure states.
- **Safe vote vs read-the-player vote** — Choose between most clue-consistent card and the card this storyteller is most likely to have chosen.
- **Steady points vs spike points** — Submit reliable decoys for frequent +1s, or high-variance decoys that could attract many votes at once.

metadata.notes: Primary source used: provided Dixit rulebook excerpt. Reformatted for scanability with explicit player-count audit headers.
